Envenomed Breath  

Description

1: Increase Poison Counter by 3
2: Decrease HP when cast by 21
3: Decrease Hitpoints by 53 per tick. Max: 90 per tick

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 173 Skill: Conjuration
Casting Time: 3 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Resist: Poison
Range: 200 Location: Any
Time of Day: Any Fizzle Adj: 25
Deletable: Yes Dot Stacking: Yes
Reflectable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: No Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Single
Spell Type: Detrimental Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You have been poisoned.
Cast on other: Soandso has been poisoned.
Effect Fades: The poison has run its course.

Game Description

Fills your target's blood with poison, causing 21 damage initially and between 53 and @3 damage every six seconds for 42 secs (7 ticks).

Items with this effect

  • Blade of the Black Dragon Eye
  • Blade of the Red Dragon Eye
  • Spell: Envenomed Breath
  • Sword of the Shissar
  • Wand of the Black Dragon Eye

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Envenomed Breath
ClassLevel
SHM24
BST35
Expansion:

Duration:
7 ticks
  • Comments (19)
  • Sold By (13)
  • NPCs with Ability (10)
  • Screenshots

  • Adnia Pashdi - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp
  • Hatem - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp

  • Firrion Roweus - 4pp 1gp 9sp 9cp

Everfrost Peaks
  • Starn Bearjumper - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp

Innothule Swamp
  • Stragak - 4pp 5gp 7sp 4cp

Shar Vahl
  • Dar Khura Ahmih - 4pp 9gp 3sp 2cp

Shar Vahl, Divided (NoS)
  • Dar Khura Ahmih - NoS - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp

The Plane of Knowledge
  • Mystic Abomin - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp
  • Primalist Worenon - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp

The Rathe Mountains
  • Scribe Garp - 3pp 1gp 2sp 4cp

  • a froglok herbalist
  • a goblin frost caller
  • a kobold advisor
  • a large undead gnoll
  • Guard Gruilka
  • Soothsayer Dregzak - Frontier Mountains
  • Soothsayer Mrugg Doxok
  • Spirit Weaver Tramix
  • Tribal Shaman
Post Comment
Beastie Conjuration skill
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2005 at 2:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1690762594804
View User Forum
Scholar
33 posts
For beasties having trouble with fizzled DoT spells (like Envenomed Breath), the answer should already be in your spell book. At level 17 you got Summon Drink and at level 29 you got Summon Food. (Come on you bought the spell, right?) Every time you log on to your beastie cast until you have a stack on 20 summoned water and 20 summoned food -- just watch your conjuration skill go up. If you have waited until level 35 to begin, you may want to spend a couple hours making bags full of summoned food. You can also practice cast you lower level DoT spells on your pet for skillups. (BTW, the pet will not be hurt by this process so no PETA flamers, please!!)
Luknu Upandown of Stromm -- 35th season Vah Shir Beastlord
▲ Page top

# Jun 25 2005 at 3:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591846630984
9 posts
i am having a ton of trouble casting this spell... i am a 41 bst with a conj skill of about 106 and i can never cast this without fizzling away 80% of my mana... is anyone else having this problem?

QuoteReply
# May 10 2005 at 10:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776535216
10 posts
It's level 39 for Beastlords now.
▲ Page top
the goodness of this spell
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2004 at 3:38 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591777083508
11 posts
As a shaman i have found this spell to be simply incredible.

After leaving the paludal caverns at about level 25, i have noticed that mobs in sucessive zones, for example netherbian lair, mseru and dawnshroud peaks had a shockingly large amount of HP compared to our old friends, the bandits of the paludal caverns.

I was faced with long fights when soloing, my melee skills being well developed and yet the mobs just would not die fast enough. I was finding the fiends in the neth lair surviving long enough for slow to wear off, and well, basicaly experience would slow down.

Enter Envenomed Breath spell. With a cheap (on most servers) burning affliction II item (serpentine bracer), affliction efficiency and haste, this little number has dramaticaly increased the rate of kills at the level that we get this spell.

While in the neth lair, i would pull with slow, then while the mob was still running, this dot, and then just melee it to death. Thus the mobs would die a lot faster.

Another great thing about this spell is helping you with adds. When meleeing one mob, and one or two mobs would add, i'd keep all adds under the effect of this rather nice DOT. Then when my main target was dead, i'd go back to the add, and it would be already significantly wounded.

Its like having a melee friend with you :)

Anothe tip i'd like to pass on to fellow shamans is to use our dots as much as possible. Sure, you might be twinked to such extent that the damage of these spells at lower levels seems insignificant when compared to your uber twinking BUT remember the school of conjuration. Our higher level DOTs benefit from having high conjuration skill. Our pets benefit from knowing conjuration.

There is a point somewhere midway through your shaman career when dots become so powerful that no matter what weapon you have (bar maybe lodestar from the cragqueen...maybe), the dots will be always way more powerful dps than your melee.

So, to sum it all up, this spell is great to speed up kills at a time when mobs have more and more hp as well as helping you work up the conjuration skill for your later shamaning career :)

good luck and good hunting.
▲ Page top
the goodness of this spell

sold on the shaman spell vendor in abysmal sea.. Hatem use ctrl F to find (abysmal sea is accessed from the magus in nedaria's landing)

Location
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2004 at 11:58 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570733061
View User Forum
12 posts
Vendor: Da Khura Ahmih
Location: -217.8, -349.4, -164.81

He is located on the second floor of the Alchemy Supply house.

With the new find window it makes it even easier now all you have to do is <Ctrl F> select Dar Khura Ahmih then find. You will be given a mystical path to the vendor.
▲ Page top
Stacking spells
QuoteReply
# Aug 31 2003 at 9:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259181964
13 posts
does the same shaman DoT spells stack? IE if I cast this 2 times on a mob will it cause twice as much damage over the 7 ticks? or is it a waste of mana to continue to cast this spell if the mob is already affected ?
▲ Page top
RE: Stacking spells
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2003 at 6:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591784748590
350 posts
No that doesnt stack.
Only spells of diffrend "schools" stack.
So the "desease" based and "poison" based whil stack. but 2 of " poison" wont
▲ Page top
RE: Stacking spells
QuoteReply
# Apr 28 2004 at 4:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1600647551617
282 posts
you can stack dot spells of the same "school" as long as they aren't the same level i.e you can stack envenomed breath, malaria, VoS, and sicken.
▲ Page top
RE: Stacking spells
QuoteReply
# Oct 14 2004 at 2:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253706228
View User Forum
Scholar
39 posts
The same spell cast by different shamen will also stack. One shaman cannot cast the same spell on one mob multiple times and have it add, but pretty much any other combination will work. This is for poison and disease - not sure about curse & epic dots
▲ Page top
proc

I have bought this spell in Shar Vahl from Dar Khura Ahmih for 17pp 5gp 6sp 2cp with a cha of 76 and ally faction.

The merchant can be found in the building near the bank on the second floor out on the balcony.

Cheers
